TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: uzai a marevaɫaw ka sitsuayan.
S.PHON: uzai a marəvaʎaw ka siʦuajan.
S.FORM: uzai a marevaljaw ka sicuayan.
S.PHON: uzai a maRəvaʎaw ka siʦuajan.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a couple,

S.FORM: uzai a uqaɫay a aɫak.
S.PHON: uzai a uqaʎaj a aʎak.
S.FORM: uzai a uqaljay a aljak.
S.PHON: uzai a uqaʎaj a aʎak.
S.TRANSL: and they had a son.

S.FORM: sa nasemvaɫ azua kina.
S.PHON: sa nasəmvaʎ azua kina.
S.FORM: sa nasemvalj azua kina.
S.PHON: sa nasəmvaʎ azua kina.
S.TRANSL: The mother was carrying the child on her back.

S.FORM: qau azua kina: "vaiku a kikasiw," ayain a kama.
S.PHON: qau azua kina: "vaiku a kikasiw," ajain a kama.
S.FORM: qau azua kina: "vaiku a kikasiw," ayain a kama.
S.PHON: qau azua kina: "vaiku a kikasiw," ajain a kama.
S.TRANSL: The mother said to the father: “Go and get some wood.”

S.FORM: qau "ui, vaik aken a kikasiw," aya a kama.
S.PHON: qau "ui, vaik akən a kikasiw," aja a kama.
S.FORM: qau "ui, vaik aken a kikasiw," aya a kama.
S.PHON: qau "ui, vaik akən a kikasiw," aja a kama.
S.TRANSL: “Right, I’m off to get wood,” said the father.

S.FORM: qau kematsu ta tsalis a laḍuaḍuq, sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau kəmaʦu ta ʦaɭis a ɭaɖuaɖuq, sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.FORM: qau kemacu ta calis a ladruadruq, sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau kəmaʦu ta ʦaɣis a ɣaɖuaɖuq, sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.TRANSL: He took a long rope, and went to get wood.

S.FORM: manu uzai a qanivung a ḍusa qapuluan.
S.PHON: manu uzai a qanivuŋ a ɖusa qapuɭuan.
S.FORM: manu uzai a qanivung a drusa qapuluan.
S.PHON: manu uzai a qanivuŋ a ɖusa qapuɣuan.
S.TRANSL: There were two phoenix bushes.

S.FORM: ini seqasi; vengetjen tua tsalis, sa vatsaqi.
S.PHON: ini səqasi; vəŋəcən tua ʦaɭis, sa vaʦaqi.
S.FORM: ini seqasi; vengetjen tua calis, sa vacaqi.
S.PHON: ini səqasi; vəŋəʦən tua ʦaɣis, sa vaʦaqi.
S.TRANSL: He didn’t cut them, but tied them with the rope, and put them on a pole to carry.

S.FORM: ɫakua ini pakakatsu.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini pakakaʦu.
S.FORM: ljakua ini pakakacu.
S.PHON: ʎakua ini pakakaʦu.
S.TRANSL: But he couldn’t carry them.

S.FORM: qau ini katsu-i a kasiw; vaik a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau ini kaʦu-i a kasiw; vaik a cumaq.
S.FORM: qau ini kacu-i a kasiw; vaik a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau ini kaʦu-i a kasiw; vaik a ʦumaq.
S.TRANSL: So he took no wood, and went off home.

S.FORM: qau sa tjumaɫ tua vaɫaw: "liaw aravats a ku kinikasiw.
S.PHON: qau sa cumaʎ tua vaʎaw: "ɭiaw aravaʦ a ku kinikasiw.
S.FORM: qau sa tjumalj tua valjaw: "liaw aravac a ku kinikasiw.
S.PHON: qau sa ʦumaʎ tua vaʎaw: "ɣiaw aRavaʦ a ku kinikasiw.
S.TRANSL: And he told his wife: “I’ve collected lots of wood.

S.FORM: ini pakakatsu aken.
S.PHON: ini pakakaʦu akən.
S.FORM: ini pakakacu aken.
S.PHON: ini pakakaʦu akən.
S.TRANSL: I couldn’t carry it.

S.FORM: vaiku a malap," ayain a vaɫaw a vavayan.
S.PHON: vaiku a maɭap," ajain a vaʎaw a vavajan.
S.FORM: vaiku a malap," ayain a valjaw a vavayan.
S.PHON: vaiku a maɣap," ajain a vaʎaw a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: Go and get it,” he said to his wife.

S.FORM: qau pazazekazekatjen nua vavayan: "aitsu a keḍikeḍi laɫa-u anga tua zaɫum, sa papavanavi anga; sa supu-i anga a djurikuku," ayain a vaɫaw a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: qau pazazəkazəkacən nua vavajan: "aiʦu a kəɖikəɖi ɭaʎa-u aŋa tua zaʎum, sa papavanavi aŋa; sa supu-i aŋa a ɉurikuku," ajain a vaʎaw a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: qau pazazekazekatjen nua vavayan: "aicu a kedrikedri lalja-u anga tua zaljum, sa papavanavi anga; sa supu-i anga a djurikuku," ayain a valjaw a uqaljay.
S.PHON: qau pazazəkazəkaʦən nua vavajan: "aiʦu a kəɖikəɖi ɣaʎa-u aŋa tua zaʎum, sa papavanavi aŋa; sa supu-i aŋa a ɉuRikuku," ajain a vaʎaw a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: The woman gave him an order: “Heat up some water for this child and bathe him; and count the chickens,” said the wife to the man.

S.FORM: qau a vaɫaw a vavayan sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau a vaʎaw a vavajan sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.FORM: qau a valjaw a vavayan sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.PHON: qau a vaʎaw a vavajan sa vaik a kikasiw.
S.TRANSL: And the wife went to get the wood.

S.FORM: qau a uqaɫay a vaɫaw laɫain a zaɫum a papinatuk, sa pataladjen a keḍikeḍi.
S.PHON: qau a uqaʎaj a vaʎaw ɭaʎain a zaʎum a papinatuk, sa pataɭaɉən a kəɖikəɖi.
S.FORM: qau a uqaljay a valjaw laljain a zaljum a papinatuk, sa pataladjen a kedrikedri.
S.PHON: qau a uqaʎaj a vaʎaw ɣaʎain a zaʎum a papinatuk, sa pataɣaɉən a kəɖikəɖi.
S.TRANSL: So the husband heated some water till it was boiling, and put the child in it.

S.FORM: navungisir anga azua keḍikeḍi a namatsay anga.
S.PHON: navuŋisir aŋa azua kəɖikəɖi a namaʦaj aŋa.
S.FORM: navungisir anga azua kedrikedri a namacay anga.
S.PHON: navuŋisiR aŋa azua kəɖikəɖi a namaʦaj aŋa.
S.TRANSL: The child died with teeth bared(?).

S.FORM: qau azua vaɫaw a uqaɫay: "masalusalu a keḍikeḍi a ku papavanavan," ayaya malevaleva tua namatsay anga a keḍikeḍi.
S.PHON: qau azua vaʎaw a uqaʎaj: "masaɭusaɭu a kəɖikəɖi a ku papavanavan," ajaja maɭəvaɭəva tua namaʦaj aŋa a kəɖikəɖi.
S.FORM: qau azua valjaw a uqaljay: "masalusalu a kedrikedri a ku papavanavan," ayaya malevaleva tua namacay anga a kedrikedri.
S.PHON: qau azua vaʎaw a uqaʎaj: "masaɣusaɣu a kəɖikəɖi a ku papavanavan," ajaja maɣəvaɣəva tua namaʦaj aŋa a kəɖikəɖi.
S.TRANSL: The husband thought: “The child is glad I’ve bathed it,” and he was happy about the dead child.

S.FORM: qau pakanan a djurikuku uta, sa djameqi, sa penipeniḍi a liqu; qau sa kelayan pitjua djeɫep.
S.PHON: qau pakanan a ɉurikuku uta, sa ɉaməqi, sa pənipəniɖi a ɭiqu; qau sa kəɭajan picua ɉəʎəp.
S.FORM: qau pakanan a djurikuku uta, sa djameqi, sa penipenidri a liqu; qau sa kelayan pitjua djeljep.
S.PHON: qau pakanan a ɉuRikuku uta, sa ɉaməqi, sa pənipəniɖi a ɣiqu; qau sa kəɣajan piʦua ɉəʎəp.
S.TRANSL: He fed the chickens too; then caught them and wrung their necks, and hung them up on the wall.

S.FORM: qau azua vavayan vaik a malap tua kinikasiw.
S.PHON: qau azua vavajan vaik a maɭap tua kinikasiw.
S.FORM: qau azua vavayan vaik a malap tua kinikasiw.
S.PHON: qau azua vavajan vaik a maɣap tua kinikasiw.
S.TRANSL: The woman went to collect the wood he had gathered.

S.FORM: manu inika kasiw; qapulu nua qanivung.
S.PHON: manu inika kasiw; qapuɭu nua qanivuŋ.
S.FORM: manu inika kasiw; qapulu nua qanivung.
S.PHON: manu inika kasiw; qapuɣu nua qanivuŋ.
S.TRANSL: But it wasn’t wood; it was whole phoenix bushes.

S.FORM: qau tsemikel a tjumaq azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmikəɭ a cumaq azua vavajan.
S.FORM: qau cemikel a tjumaq azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmikəɣ a ʦumaq azua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: So she returned home.

S.FORM: "su sinupu anga a djurikuku?" ayain a kivadaq a vaɫaw a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: "su sinupu aŋa a ɉurikuku" ajain a kivadaq a vaʎaw a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: "su sinupu anga a djurikuku?" ayain a kivadaq a valjaw a uqaljay.
S.PHON: "su sinupu aŋa a ɉuRikuku" ajain a kivadaq a vaʎaw a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: “Have you counted the chickens?” she asked her husband.

S.FORM: "sinupu anga azua ku sinikelay," ayain.
S.PHON: "sinupu aŋa azua ku sinikəɭaj," ajain.
S.FORM: "sinupu anga azua ku sinikelay," ayain.
S.PHON: "sinupu aŋa azua ku sinikəɣaj," ajain.
S.TRANSL: “I’ve counted the ones I’ve hung up,” he told her.

S.FORM: qenetjan nua vavayan; namatsay anga a djurikuku a penuɫat.
S.PHON: qənəcan nua vavajan; namaʦaj aŋa a ɉurikuku a pənuʎat.
S.FORM: qenetjan nua vavayan; namacay anga a djurikuku a penuljat.
S.PHON: qənəʦan nua vavajan; namaʦaj aŋa a ɉuRikuku a pənuʎat.
S.TRANSL: The woman had a look; all the chickens were dead.

S.FORM: limutseng aravats a vavayan.
S.PHON: ɭimuʦəŋ aravaʦ a vavajan.
S.FORM: limuceng aravac a vavayan.
S.PHON: ɣimuʦəŋ aRavaʦ a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The woman was very angry.

S.FORM: qau kivadaqan uta: "pinapavanavan anga a keḍikeḍi?" ayain a kivadaq.
S.PHON: qau kivadaqan uta: "pinapavanavan aŋa a kəɖikəɖi" ajain a kivadaq.
S.FORM: qau kivadaqan uta: "pinapavanavan anga a kedrikedri?" ayain a kivadaq.
S.PHON: qau kivadaqan uta: "pinapavanavan aŋa a kəɖikəɖi" ajain a kivadaq.
S.TRANSL: She also asked: “Has the child had a bath?” she asked him.

S.FORM: "ui, ku pinapavanavan anga; masalu aravats, djemelidjeli a naqemereng," aya tua namatsay anga.
S.PHON: "ui, ku pinapavanavan aŋa; masaɭu aravaʦ, ɉəməɭiɉəɭi a naqəmərəŋ," aja tua namaʦaj aŋa.
S.FORM: "ui, ku pinapavanavan anga; masalu aravac, djemelidjeli a naqemereng," aya tua namacay anga.
S.PHON: "ui, ku pinapavanavan aŋa; masaɣu aRavaʦ, ɉəməɣiɉəɣi a naqəməRəŋ," aja tua namaʦaj aŋa.
S.TRANSL: “Yes, I’ve bathed him. He liked it very much; he’s lying there smiling,” he said about the dead child.

S.FORM: vaiken a kiqenetj nua vavayan.
S.PHON: vaikən a kiqənəc nua vavajan.
S.FORM: vaiken a kiqenetj nua vavayan.
S.PHON: vaikən a kiqənəʦ nua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The woman went to look at it.

S.FORM: mintu namatsay anga.
S.PHON: mintu namaʦaj aŋa.
S.FORM: mintu namacay anga.
S.PHON: mintu namaʦaj aŋa.
S.TRANSL: And it was dead.

S.FORM: puvarung aravats azua vavayan; qemaung anga.
S.PHON: puvaruŋ aravaʦ azua vavajan; qəmauŋ aŋa.
S.FORM: puvarung aravac azua vavayan; qemaung anga.
S.PHON: puvaRuŋ aRavaʦ azua vavajan; qəmauŋ aŋa.
S.TRANSL: The woman was very upset, and cried.

S.FORM: qau kiɫavaran a uqaɫay: "aku qemaung sun a paqeteleng?
S.PHON: qau kiʎavaran a uqaʎaj: "aku qəmauŋ sun a paqətəɭəŋ
S.FORM: qau kiljavaran a uqaljay: "aku qemaung sun a paqeteleng?
S.PHON: qau kiʎavaRan a uqaʎaj: "aku qəmauŋ sun a paqətəɣəŋ
S.TRANSL: The man said: “Why are you being so silly as to cry?

S.FORM: a djemelidjeli a keḍikeḍi."
S.PHON: a ɉəməɭiɉəɭi a kəɖikəɖi."
S.FORM: a djemelidjeli a kedrikedri."
S.PHON: a ɉəməɣiɉəɣi a kəɖikəɖi."
S.TRANSL: The child is smiling.”

S.FORM: kiɫavaran a vavayan: "aya itjen tua namatsay anga?" aya.
S.PHON: kiʎavaran a vavajan: "aja icən tua namaʦaj aŋa" aja.
S.FORM: kiljavaran a vavayan: "aya itjen tua namacay anga?" aya.
S.PHON: kiʎavaRan a vavajan: "aja iʦən tua namaʦaj aŋa" aja.
S.TRANSL: The woman said: “Do we talk like that about the dead?”

S.FORM: qau sa qaung anga azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sa qauŋ aŋa azua vavajan.
S.FORM: qau sa qaung anga azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sa qauŋ aŋa azua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: And the woman cried.

S.FORM: qau tsuay anga, "alapu tua titaw katua pungki," ayain a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaj aŋa, "aɭapu tua titaw katua puŋki," ajain a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: qau cuay anga, "alapu tua titaw katua pungki," ayain a uqaljay.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaj aŋa, "aɣapu tua titaw katua puŋki," ajain a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: After a time she said to the man: “Take a hoe and basket.

S.FORM: qau vaik a malap tua titaw katua pungki; sa vaik tiamadju a sema tsemtsemel.
S.PHON: qau vaik a maɭap tua titaw katua puŋki; sa vaik tiamaɉu a səma ʦəmʦəməɭ.
S.FORM: qau vaik a malap tua titaw katua pungki; sa vaik tiamadju a sema cemcemel.
S.PHON: qau vaik a maɣap tua titaw katua puŋki; sa vaik tiamaɉu a səma ʦəmʦəməɣ.
S.TRANSL: So he went and got a hoe and basket; and they went off into the woods.

S.FORM: qau "kali-u tsu a imaza," ayain.
S.PHON: qau "kaɭi-u ʦu a imaza," ajain.
S.FORM: qau "kali-u cu a imaza," ayain.
S.PHON: qau "kaɣi-u ʦu a imaza," ajain.
S.TRANSL: “Dig here,” she told him.

S.FORM: kaliin nua uqaɫay.
S.PHON: kaɭiin nua uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: kaliin nua uqaljay.
S.PHON: kaɣiin nua uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: The man dug there.

S.FORM: qau sa muri tseveɫen azua keḍikeḍi.
S.PHON: qau sa muri ʦəvəʎən azua kəɖikəɖi.
S.FORM: qau sa muri ceveljen azua kedrikedri.
S.PHON: qau sa muRi ʦəvəʎən azua kəɖikəɖi.
S.TRANSL: The child was to be buried.

S.FORM: manu magarang a uqaɫay: "aku tseveɫen a paqeteleng, a masalusalu a djemelidjeli a keḍikeḍi?"
S.PHON: manu magaraŋ a uqaʎaj: "aku ʦəvəʎən a paqətəɭəŋ, a masaɭusaɭu a ɉəməɭiɉəɭi a kəɖikəɖi"
S.FORM: manu magarang a uqaljay: "aku ceveljen a paqeteleng, a masalusalu a djemelidjeli a kedrikedri?"
S.PHON: manu magaRaŋ a uqaʎaj: "aku ʦəvəʎən a paqətəɣəŋ, a masaɣusaɣu a ɉəməɣiɉəɣi a kəɖikəɖi"
S.TRANSL: But the man was angry: “Why are you being so silly as to bury him? The child is happy and smiling.”

S.FORM: "namatsay anga," ayain nua vavayan.
S.PHON: "namaʦaj aŋa," ajain nua vavajan.
S.FORM: "namacay anga," ayain nua vavayan.
S.PHON: "namaʦaj aŋa," ajain nua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: “He’s dead,” the woman told him.

S.FORM: qau tseveɫen anga nimadju.
S.PHON: qau ʦəvəʎən aŋa nimaɉu.
S.FORM: qau ceveljen anga nimadju.
S.PHON: qau ʦəvəʎən aŋa nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: And she buried him.

S.FORM: qau sa tulu-i anga azua uqaɫay: "nu mapuvaruvarung a tja salasaladj, nu qemauqaung, tja pusaladjan a mapuvarung a qemaung, saka nangaq."
S.PHON: qau sa tuɭu-i aŋa azua uqaʎaj: "nu mapuvaruvaruŋ a ca saɭasaɭaɉ, nu qəmauqauŋ, ca pusaɭaɉan a mapuvaruŋ a qəmauŋ, saka naŋaq."
S.FORM: qau sa tulu-i anga azua uqaljay: "nu mapuvaruvarung a tja salasaladj, nu qemauqaung, tja pusaladjan a mapuvarung a qemaung, saka nangaq."
S.PHON: qau sa tuɣu-i aŋa azua uqaʎaj: "nu mapuvaRuvaRuŋ a ʦa saɣasaɣaɉ, nu qəmauqauŋ, ʦa pusaɣaɉan a mapuvaRuŋ a qəmauŋ, saka naŋaq."
S.TRANSL: Then the man was taught: “When our friends are upset and cry, we join them in being upset and crying; that’s the right thing to do.”

S.FORM: qau tsuatsuay anga manu uzai a pakaikaiv.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaʦuaj aŋa manu uzai a pakaikaiv.
S.FORM: qau cuacuay anga manu uzai a pakaikaiv.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaʦuaj aŋa manu uzai a pakaikaiv.
S.TRANSL: After a time some people gave a wedding supper,

S.FORM: qau vaik a kiqepu tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau vaik a kiqəpu tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau vaik a kiqepu tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau vaik a kiqəpu tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: and they went to it.

S.FORM: manu qemaung azua pinakaivan a vavayan.
S.PHON: manu qəmauŋ azua pinakaivan a vavajan.
S.FORM: manu qemaung azua pinakaivan a vavayan.
S.PHON: manu qəmauŋ azua pinakaivan a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The bride for whom the supper was given cried;

S.FORM: mintu qemaung uta azua uqaɫay.
S.PHON: mintu qəmauŋ uta azua uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: mintu qemaung uta azua uqaljay.
S.PHON: mintu qəmauŋ uta azua uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: and then the man cried too.

S.FORM: qau kirivuan nua vaɫaw a vavayan: "aku qemaung sun a paqeteleng?" ayain.
S.PHON: qau kirivuan nua vaʎaw a vavajan: "aku qəmauŋ sun a paqətəɭəŋ" ajain.
S.FORM: qau kirivuan nua valjaw a vavayan: "aku qemaung sun a paqeteleng?" ayain.
S.PHON: qau kiRivuan nua vaʎaw a vavajan: "aku qəmauŋ sun a paqətəɣəŋ" ajain.
S.TRANSL: He was told off by his wife: “Why are you being so silly as to cry?” she said to him.

S.FORM: "'nu qemauqaung a qaliqali, qemaung itjen a pusaladj,' aya sun.
S.PHON: "'nu qəmauqauŋ a qaɭiqaɭi, qəmauŋ icən a pusaɭaɉ,' aja sun.
S.FORM: "'nu qemauqaung a qaliqali, qemaung itjen a pusaladj,' aya sun.
S.PHON: "ʔnu qəmauqauŋ a qaɣiqaɣi, qəmauŋ iʦən a pusaɣaɉ,ʔ aja sun.
S.TRANSL: “You said: ‘When other people cry, we join them in crying.’

S.FORM: aku kirivu sun a paqeteleng?" ayain a vavayan.
S.PHON: aku kirivu sun a paqətəɭəŋ" ajain a vavajan.
S.FORM: aku kirivu sun a paqeteleng?" ayain a vavayan.
S.PHON: aku kiRivu sun a paqətəɣəŋ" ajain a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: So why on earth are you scolding me?” he said to her.

S.FORM: qau keman ta tsengeɫ.
S.PHON: qau kəman ta ʦəŋəʎ.
S.FORM: qau keman ta cengelj.
S.PHON: qau kəman ta ʦəŋəʎ.
S.TRANSL: Once they were eating lunch.

S.FORM: nu paɫetekutekuin, puɫapuɫaten a djemamay nua uqaɫay.
S.PHON: nu paʎətəkutəkuin, puʎapuʎatən a ɉəmamaj nua uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: nu paljetekutekuin, puljapuljaten a djemamay nua uqaljay.
S.PHON: nu paʎətəkutəkuin, puʎapuʎatən a ɉəmamaj nua uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: When the food was being put on the table, the man ate up all the side dishes.

S.FORM: qau masiaq azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau masiaq azua vavajan.
S.FORM: qau masiaq azua vavayan.
S.PHON: qau masiaq azua vavajan.
S.TRANSL: The woman was ashamed.

S.FORM: "maya djemamay aravats.
S.PHON: "maja ɉəmamaj aravaʦ.
S.FORM: "maya djemamay aravac.
S.PHON: "maja ɉəmamaj aRavaʦ.
S.TRANSL: “Don’t eat so much of the side dishes.

S.FORM: ku qadjayay a su lima.
S.PHON: ku qaɉajaj a su ɭima.
S.FORM: ku qadjayay a su lima.
S.PHON: ku qaɉajaj a su ɣima.
S.TRANSL: I’ll tie a string round your arm.

S.FORM: nu ku tsiḍtsiḍen, djamayu," ayain.
S.PHON: nu ku ʦiɖʦiɖən, ɉamaju," ajain.
S.FORM: nu ku cidrcidren, djamayu," ayain.
S.PHON: nu ku ʦiɖʦiɖən, ɉamaju," ajain.
S.TRANSL: When I tug it, you can eat some,” she said to him.

S.FORM: nu tsuay anga ini tsiḍtsiḍi.
S.PHON: nu ʦuaj aŋa ini ʦiɖʦiɖi.
S.FORM: nu cuay anga ini cidrcidri.
S.PHON: nu ʦuaj aŋa ini ʦiɖʦiɖi.
S.TRANSL: For a long time there was no tug.

S.FORM: "aku ini anga su tsiḍtsiḍtsiḍi?" aya a uqaɫay.
S.PHON: "aku ini aŋa su ʦiɖʦiɖʦiɖi" aja a uqaʎaj.
S.FORM: "aku ini anga su cidrcidrcidri?" aya a uqaljay.
S.PHON: "aku ini aŋa su ʦiɖʦiɖʦiɖi" aja a uqaʎaj.
S.TRANSL: “Why haven’t you tugged yet?” said the man.

S.FORM: manu tjemala tideq sa paqaquɫuts a vatu.
S.PHON: manu cəmaɭa tidəq sa paqaquʎuʦ a vatu.
S.FORM: manu tjemala tideq sa paqaquljuc a vatu.
S.PHON: manu ʦəmaɣa tidəq sa paqaquʎuʦ a vatu.
S.TRANSL: Then some dogs came into the space between them and started a tussle;

S.FORM: qau matsiḍtsiḍ a lima.
S.PHON: qau maʦiɖʦiɖ a ɭima.
S.FORM: qau macidrcidr a lima.
S.PHON: qau maʦiɖʦiɖ a ɣima.
S.TRANSL: and so his arm got tugged.

S.FORM: tjadjaɫaw anan a djemamay.
S.PHON: caɉaʎaw anan a ɉəmamaj.
S.FORM: tjadjaljaw anan a djemamay.
S.PHON: ʦaɉaʎaw anan a ɉəmamaj.
S.TRANSL: He went at the side dishes even more crazily (?even quicker).

S.FORM: qau puɫaten anga a djamay.
S.PHON: qau puʎatən aŋa a ɉamaj.
S.FORM: qau puljaten anga a djamay.
S.PHON: qau puʎatən aŋa a ɉamaj.
S.TRANSL: He ate up all the side dishes;

S.FORM: nekanga nu sidjamay nua qaliqali.
S.PHON: nəkaŋa nu siɉamaj nua qaɭiqaɭi.
S.FORM: nekanga nu sidjamay nua qaliqali.
S.PHON: nəkaŋa nu siɉamaj nua qaɣiqaɣi.
S.TRANSL: there was nothing left for anyone else.
